next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 18

Nearly three hours after the commander told them the threshold might fail, Vel and Kem had two hundred meters left and nowhere decent to hide.

The sun had slid west. Long ridge shadows striped the basin floor. Heat still leaked out of the layered rock, but the hard white glare from before was gone. The commander had spent those hours doing the same thing over and over: cutting every clean line west and forcing them sideways, keeping any level they burned too far from the threshold to matter. Vel had answered with patience, bad footing, and blood instead of spending.

She crouched behind a rib of sandstone that covered half a shoulder if a person believed in miracles. Her left ankle hurt from a bad landing. Her mouth tasted like old dust. Kem lay three meters off her left knee, breathing through his mouth, chin already bloodied from one earlier fall, still at twelve. She still had eight. Four Reserve agents held the basin. Two garrison fighters ahead. The blink-step specialist circling south. The commander at the point itself, patient as drought.

High on the north scree, gravel hissed down in a narrow, steady ribbon. Too regular for wind. The commander looked that direction once and looked away. She kept every agent on the basin floor. Nobody could break from the line to investigate the ridge and still keep Vel and Kem off the point.

"You hear me say spend, you spend," Vel said.

Kem wiped blood off his mouth with the back of his hand. "And if you don't?"

"You use your body."

He nodded once. "I can — yeah. Okay."

"Only plan we've got."

She touched the ro...
open_hooks_to_carry: The Scour as landscape: vast erosion basin, exposed rock, hostile terrain
Red Reserve permanent garrison: a commander who has guarded the site for five years, witnessed three crossings, knows what zero is — plus two freshly deployed agents for the Kem retrieval; four total

The late complication: the commander reveals the threshold has degraded — natural erosion compounded by three years of deliberate dampening; a two-person cascade may not be enough; Vel's eight-year-old calculations are outdated
Vel at 16 and Kem at 18: the numbers against them rendered in dark comedy — 'I'm finally discreet enough for the Conservatory Rite'
The tunnel approach: Kem spends 6 levels (light, reinforcement, defensive), Vel spends 6 (Perception mapping, tunnel navigation, evasion); Vel at 8, Kem at 12
The commander as mirror-Sable: competent, spent, loyal to a flawed system, guarding something she knows is real because the alternative would collapse everything
Intercut: Sable trailing three hours behind at 236, spending on tracking, making incremental decisions toward the Scour

payoffs_to_land.description: The permanent Scour garrison is confirmed — the commander has been here for years, knows the threshold intimately, and has standing orders to prevent crossings

prev_tail_excerpt:
...around a dead settlement where Shaping-built walls were still standing without roofs. A notice clung to what had once been a community board: PRODUCTIVITY TIER ASSESSMENT. ALL UNITS REPORT TO SECTOR ADMINISTRATION FOR ANNUAL OUTPUT REVIEW. Someone had written underneath it in a different hand: *They left. We're still here. The notice is still here too.*

By the time they reached a communal well, Kem was still walking beside her instead of ten feet ahead. He filled both canteens without comment and handed hers over. Fingers touched on the metal and moved on.

Vel put the journal back in her pack after that. Maren's cramped handwriting rode against her spine again. There were two more days in it, maybe a little less if the road held. After that either the journal had been a map or it had been a relic.

The road dipped over an old mine gallery. Vel stepped around the depression. Kem matched the line without thinking and kept talking.

"If the Reserve has four," he said, looking ahead, "and we have thirty-two between us, that's bad."

"Yes."

"How bad."

"Bad enough that I don't want the first surprise to be there."

"Fine." He adjusted his pack. "Then no surprises."

Fourteen hummed at the edge of her awareness. Eighteen hummed at his. Thirty-two lay between them and a permanent garrison at the Scour.

Dren was somewhere north with a ruined left arm, a hundred and twenty levels, and an eight-year-old daughter waiting for a healer to make her legs hold. Vel kept seeing his face when he'd said *my girl's my zero.*

The arithmetic ahead was still terrible. It was just honest now.

setups_to_plant.description: The threshold has been deliberately weakened — a two-person cascade may not generate enough energy. This creates a structural necessity for a third contributor, making Sable's arrival essential rather than convenient.

state_deltas_required: Near-zero levels vs. dangerous terrain and trained Red Reserve garrison; the late complication that the threshold has degraded

Every remaining ability becomes life-or-death; the late complication reveals the threshold has degraded and two spenders may not be enough; the Hoarded's active suppression is confirmed; Sable's approach seeded through incremental decisions
